items.content_statement: Young people understand the correct terms for different parts of the body
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual content about anatomical terminology that must be held

items.content_statement: Young people are able to confidently use correct terms for different parts of the body
items.classification_rationale: Procedural skill - specific capability to use anatomical terms when situations call for it
prerequisite_lts: Young people understand the correct terms for different parts of the body

items.content_statement: skills and knowledge that form the building blocks of all positive relationships
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about what constitutes foundational relationship skills - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: know how to keep themselves and others safe
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when safety situations arise - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning across contexts

items.content_statement: grow into kind, caring adults who have respect for others
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientations that exist only as enacted across time and contexts - kindness, caring, and respect as lived qualities
prerequisite_lts: skills and knowledge that form the building blocks of all positive relationships

items.content_statement: skills for managing difficult feelings in their friendships, like disappointment or anger
items.classification_rationale: occasion-triggered analytical capability deployed when difficult feelings arise in friendship contexts

items.content_statement: reflect on how to behave with kindness in more complex or challenging relationships
items.classification_rationale: analytical reasoning capability deployed when navigating relationship challenges
prerequisite_lts: skills for managing difficult feelings in their friendships, like disappointment or anger

items.content_statement: Many forms of families provide a nurturing environment for children, and can include single parent families, same-sex parents, families headed by grandparents, young carers, kinship carers, adoptive parents and foster parents/carers.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about family structures - factual content about what constitutes nurturing family environments

items.content_statement: Care should be taken to ensure that children are not stigmatised based on their home circumstances.
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation - ongoing default practice of avoiding stigmatisation across contexts
prerequisite_lts: Many forms of families provide a nurturing environment for children, and can include single parent families, same-sex parents, families headed by grandparents, young carers, kinship carers, adoptive parents and foster parents/carers.

items.content_statement: positive relationships, boundaries, privacy, and children's rights over their own bodies and personal information
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about concepts that must be understood before skills can be applied

items.content_statement: recognise emotional, physical and sexual abuse
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when situations call for recognition and assessment
prerequisite_lts: positive relationships, boundaries, privacy, and children's rights over their own bodies and personal information

items.content_statement: recognise and report risks and abuse, including online
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when situations call for risk assessment and appropriate response
prerequisite_lts: positive relationships, boundaries, privacy, and children's rights over their own bodies and personal information

items.content_statement: keep themselves and others safe
items.classification_rationale: sustained orientation that operates by default across contexts as an ongoing protective stance
prerequisite_lts: positive relationships, boundaries, privacy, and children's rights over their own bodies and personal information
recognise emotional, physical and sexual abuse
recognise and report risks and abuse, including online

items.content_statement: Being a victim of abuse is never the fault of the child
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - a factual claim about responsibility and blame that learners must understand

items.content_statement: Families can provide love, security and stability for children growing up safe and happy
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual claim about how families function that learners must understand

items.content_statement: The characteristics of safe and happy family life, including commitment to each other in times of difficulty, protection and care for children and other family members, the importance of spending time together and sharing each other's lives
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about family characteristics - factual/conceptual content about what constitutes safe and happy family life

items.content_statement: that the families of other children, either in school or in the wider world, sometimes look different from their family, but that other children's families are also characterised by love and care
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about family diversity and commonalities - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: respect those differences in other children's families
items.classification_rationale: sustained orientation of respect enacted across contexts - exists only as practised behaviour over time
prerequisite_lts: that the families of other children, either in school or in the wider world, sometimes look different from their family, but that other children's families are also characterised by love and care

items.content_statement: That stable, caring relationships are at the heart of safe and happy families and are important for children's security as they grow up.
items.classification_rationale: This is a propositional claim about how relationships function and their effects on children - factual/conceptual content to be understood, not a skill to deploy or orientation to sustain.

items.content_statement: That marriage and civil partnerships represent a formal and legally recognised commitment of two people to each other which is intended to be lifelong.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the legal and social nature of marriage and civil partnerships - factual content about how these institutions are defined and function

items.content_statement: how to recognise if family relationships are making them feel unhappy or unsafe, and how to seek help or advice from others if needed
items.classification_rationale: occasion-triggered analytical capability - deployed when situations call for recognition and response to unsafe family dynamics

items.content_statement: How people choose and make friends
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about social processes - conceptual understanding of friendship formation mechanisms

items.content_statement: That healthy friendships are positive and welcoming towards others, and do not make others feel lonely or excluded
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about characteristics of healthy friendships - factual/conceptual content to be understood

items.content_statement: Skills for developing caring, kind friendships
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered analytical/social skills deployed when friendship-building situations arise

items.content_statement: That most people feel lonely sometimes, and that there is no shame in feeling lonely or talking about it.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about emotional experiences and social norms - factual content about loneliness being common and acceptable to discuss

items.content_statement: The characteristics of friendships that lead to happiness and security, including mutual respect, honesty, trustworthiness, loyalty, kindness, generosity, trust, sharing interests and experiences, and support with problems and difficulties
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about friendship characteristics - factual content about what constitutes healthy friendships

items.content_statement: Most friendships have ups and downs, and that these can often be worked through so that the friendship is repaired or even strengthened.
items.classification_rationale: This is propositional knowledge about how friendships work - a factual/conceptual claim about the nature of relationships that learners must understand.

items.content_statement: how to recognise when a friendship is making them feel unhappy or uncomfortable, and how to get support when needed
items.classification_rationale: occasion-triggered analytical capability - deployed when situations call for recognising problematic friendship dynamics and seeking appropriate support

items.content_statement: How to balance the needs and wishes of different people in relationships and why this can be complicated
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability across multiple lenses - requires reasoning about competing needs, multiple valid approaches exist, quality of reasoning determines assessment outcome

items.content_statement: Setting and respecting healthy boundaries in relationships with friends, family, peers and adults
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained default orientation that operates across multiple relationship contexts over time, requiring multi-informant observation of enacted behaviour

items.content_statement: how to be assertive and express needs and boundaries
items.classification_rationale: occasion-triggered analytical skill - deployed when situations call for assertive communication; multiple valid approaches exist; reasoning quality determines effectiveness

items.content_statement: how to manage feelings, including disappointment and frustration
items.classification_rationale: occasion-triggered analytical skill - deployed when specific emotional situations arise; multiple valid strategies exist; reasoning quality determines appropriateness of response

items.content_statement: The difference between being assertive and being controlling, and conversely the difference between being kind to other people and neglecting your own needs
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about conceptual distinctions that can be understood and explained

items.content_statement: respecting others, including those who are different (for example, physically, in character, personality or backgrounds), or make different choices, or have different preferences or beliefs
items.classification_rationale: describes a sustained orientation that operates by default across contexts - respecting others is an enacted capacity that exists only as it is demonstrated over time in real interactions

items.content_statement: Take practical steps and develop skills in a range of different contexts to improve or support their relationships
items.classification_rationale: Describes analytical/reasoning capability deployed when situations call for relationship improvement or support - occasion-triggered skill across multiple contexts

items.content_statement: think about how they foster their own self-esteem and build a strong sense of their own identity, including through developing skills and interests
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when occasions call for self-reflection - Type 2

items.content_statement: responsibilities of bystanders (primarily reporting bullying to an adult), and how to get help
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about specific responsibilities and procedures that can be factually assessed

items.content_statement: What a stereotype is, how stereotypes can be unfair, negative, destructive or lead to bullying
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the definition and effects of stereotypes - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: How to challenge a stereotype
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - reasoning about how to respond to stereotypes in specific contexts
prerequisite_lts: What a stereotype is, how stereotypes can be unfair, negative, destructive or lead to bullying

items.content_statement: How to seek help when needed, including when they are concerned about violence, harm, or when they are unsure who to trust.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when specific situations arise - requires reasoning about context, available resources, and appropriate responses across different scenarios

items.content_statement: That people should be respectful in online interactions, and that the same principles apply to online relationships as to face-to-face relationships, including where people are anonymous
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how principles transfer across contexts - factual/conceptual content to be understood

items.content_statement: Strategies for avoiding putting pressure on others to share information and images online, and strategies for resisting peer pressure
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when specific online situations call for it - occasion-triggered skills for navigating social pressures
prerequisite_lts: That people should be respectful in online interactions, and that the same principles apply to online relationships as to face-to-face relationships, including where people are anonymous

items.content_statement: awareness of the risks associated with people they have never met, including that people sometimes behave differently online, including pretending to be someone else, or pretending to be a child, and that this can lead to dangerous situations
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about online risks and deceptive behaviors - factual content about how the digital world works

items.content_statement: how to critically evaluate their online relationships and sources of information
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when encountering online relationships or information - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning quality

items.content_statement: how to recognise harmful content or harmful contact, and how to report this
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when encountering potentially harmful online situations - occasion-triggered skill requiring recognition and response

items.content_statement: That there is a minimum age for joining social media sites (currently 13), which protects children from inappropriate content or unsafe contact with older social media users, who may be strangers, including other children and adults.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how age restrictions function as protective measures - factual content about the purpose and mechanism of social media age limits

items.content_statement: Understanding the importance of privacy and location settings to protect information online
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how privacy settings function to protect information - factual/conceptual content

items.content_statement: The importance of exercising caution about sharing any information about themselves online
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientation of caution in online sharing behavior - operates across contexts as enacted practice
prerequisite_lts: Understanding the importance of privacy and location settings to protect information online

items.content_statement: that once a picture or words has been circulated there is no way of deleting it everywhere and no control over where it ends up
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about how digital content behaves once shared - factual claim about the world that learners must understand

items.content_statement: where to go for advice and support when they feel worried or concerned about something they have seen or engaged with online
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about specific resources and procedures - hard prerequisite sequence applies as children must know the factual information about where help is available

items.content_statement: What sorts of boundaries are appropriate in friendships with peers and others (including online). This can include learning about boundaries in play and in negotiations about space, toys, books, resources etc.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about appropriate boundaries - conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation or application to scenarios

items.content_statement: that it is not always right to keep secrets if they relate to being safe
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about when secret-keeping is inappropriate - a conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation or scenario application

items.content_statement: the differences between appropriate and inappropriate or unsafe contact, including physical contact
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about definitional distinctions that must be understood - Type 1 conceptual content

items.content_statement: How to respond safely and appropriately to adults they may encounter (in all contexts, including online), including those they do and do not know
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when encountering adults - requires reasoning about context, relationship, and appropriate response strategies across multiple situations

items.content_statement: recognising who to trust and who not to trust
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for trust judgments - multiple valid frameworks exist for evaluating trustworthiness, reasoning quality determines assessment outcome

items.content_statement: The vocabulary needed to report abuse, concerns about something seen online or experienced in real life, or feelings of being unsafe or feeling bad about any adult.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - specific vocabulary that must be learned

items.content_statement: How to report abuse, concerns about something seen online or experienced in real life, or feelings of being unsafe or feeling bad about any adult.
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered skill - deployed when a specific situation calls for reporting

items.content_statement: The confidence needed to report abuse, concerns about something seen online or experienced in real life, or feelings of being unsafe or feeling bad about any adult.
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation - confidence as an enacted capacity that operates by default across contexts

items.content_statement: Where to get advice e.g. family, school and/or other sources
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about factual information - specific sources and locations for advice

items.content_statement: Sex education is not compulsory in primary schools, but is recommended in years 5 and/or 6, in line with content about conception and birth, which forms part of the national curriculum for science
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about curriculum policy and recommendations - factual content about what is compulsory vs recommended

items.content_statement: The national curriculum for science includes subject content in related areas, such as the main external body parts, the human body as it grows from birth to old age (including puberty) and reproduction in some plants and animals
items.classification_rationale: Factual knowledge about what is included in the national curriculum for science - propositional content about curriculum requirements

items.content_statement: Schools may also cover human reproduction in the science curriculum, but where they do so, this should be in line with the factual description of conception in the science curriculum
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about curriculum policy - factual content about how schools may approach human reproduction content and the requirement for alignment with science curriculum

items.content_statement: Primary schools should consult parents about the content of anything that will be taught within sex education
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about required school procedures - factual content about what schools must do

items.content_statement: Schools should offer parents support in talking to their children about sex education and how to link this with what is being taught in school
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about required school support services - factual content about what schools should provide

items.content_statement: Parents have the right to request withdrawal from sex education
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about parental rights - factual content about legal entitlements

items.content_statement: Knowledge needed in later life to keep themselves and others safe, and how to avoid sexually transmitted infections and unplanned pregnancies
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about safety and health information - Type 1 factual content

items.content_statement: Develop healthy, safe and nurturing relationships of all kinds
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation toward relationship quality that operates by default across contexts - Type 3 dispositional capacity
prerequisite_lts: Knowledge needed in later life to keep themselves and others safe, and how to avoid sexually transmitted infections and unplanned pregnancies

items.content_statement: RSE will enable young people to make their own choices about whether or when to develop safe, fulfilling and healthy sexual relationships, once they reach the age of consent, and to resist pressure to have sex
items.classification_rationale: Describes sustained orientations that operate by default across contexts - making autonomous choices about relationships and resisting pressure - rather than occasion-triggered skills

items.content_statement: discuss and critically evaluate complex relationship scenarios
items.classification_rationale: occasion-triggered analytical capability deployed when relationship scenarios are presented for evaluation

items.content_statement: Being a victim of abuse is never the fault of the child or young person
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual claim about responsibility and blame that learners must understand

items.content_statement: Different forms of abuse exist and should be addressed sensitively and clearly at appropriate ages
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual understanding about types of abuse and developmental appropriateness

items.content_statement: Schools have an important role as a place of consistency and safety where pupils can find support, particularly for those experiencing unhealthy or unsafe relationships
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding - transferable idea about the protective function of schools in safeguarding contexts

items.content_statement: Use approaches such as distancing techniques, setting ground rules with the class to help manage sensitive discussion, and using question boxes to allow pupils to raise issues anonymously when teaching sensitive topics
items.classification_rationale: Describes analytical capability deployed when a situation calls for it (teaching sensitive topics) - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning about appropriate approaches

items.content_statement: The legal status of marriage and civil partnership, including that they carry legal rights, benefits and protections that are not available to couples who are cohabiting or who have, for example, undergone a non-legally binding religious ceremony.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal facts and distinctions - factual content about how legal systems work that learners must hold

items.content_statement: Cohabitants do not obtain marriage-like status or rights from living together or by having children
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual claim about legal status that learners must understand

items.content_statement: How families and relationships change over time, including through birth, death, separation and new relationships
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about family lifecycle patterns and relationship transitions - factual/conceptual content to be understood

items.content_statement: characteristics of successful parenting
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about factual characteristics that can be learned and assessed through knowledge check

items.content_statement: the importance of the early years of a child's life for brain development
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding of causal relationship between early years and brain development

items.content_statement: Judge when a relationship is unsafe and where to seek help when needed, including when pupils are concerned about violence, harm, or when they are unsure who to trust
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - requires reasoning across multiple factors (violence indicators, harm assessment, trust evaluation) with no single correct answer but better and worse judgments

items.content_statement: The role of consent, trust, mutual respect, honesty, kindness, loyalty, shared interests and outlooks, generosity, boundaries, tolerance, privacy, and the management of conflict, reconciliation and ending relationships in romantic relationships
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how relationship elements function - conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation of roles and connections

items.content_statement: treat others with kindness and respect, including in public spaces and including strangers
items.classification_rationale: sustained default orientation - treating others with kindness and respect operates by default across contexts, not deployed when prompted
prerequisite_lts: understand the legal rights and responsibilities regarding equality, and that everyone is unique and equal

items.content_statement: understand the legal rights and responsibilities regarding equality, and that everyone is unique and equal
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge - factual/conceptual claims about legal rights, responsibilities, and equality principles

items.content_statement: The importance of self-esteem, independence and having a positive relationship with oneself, and how these characteristics support healthy relationships with others
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how self-esteem and independence affect relationship quality

items.content_statement: Developing one's own interests, hobbies, friendship groups, and skills
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation - ongoing development of personal interests and social connections across contexts

items.content_statement: What it means to be treated with respect by others
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of respect in relationships - definitional knowledge

items.content_statement: skills for communicating respectfully within relationships and with strangers, including in situations of conflict
items.classification_rationale: occasion-triggered analytical capability deployed when communication situations arise; multiple valid approaches exist; reasoning quality determines effectiveness

items.content_statement: The different types of bullying (including online bullying)
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about categories and definitions - hard prerequisite sequence where understanding types is foundational

items.content_statement: The impact of bullying
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of causal relationships and consequences - builds on knowing what bullying is
prerequisite_lts: The different types of bullying (including online bullying)

items.content_statement: How and where to get help regarding bullying
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a bullying situation arises - requires reasoning about context and appropriate responses
prerequisite_lts: The different types of bullying (including online bullying)
The impact of bullying

items.content_statement: Responsibilities of bystanders to report bullying
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation toward taking responsibility as a bystander - exists only as enacted across contexts over time
prerequisite_lts: The different types of bullying (including online bullying)
The impact of bullying

items.content_statement: Skills for ending relationships or friendships with kindness and managing the difficult feelings that endings might bring, including disappointment, hurt or frustration
items.classification_rationale: Analytical/reasoning capability deployed when a relationship ending situation arises - multiple valid approaches exist, quality of reasoning and emotional management determines outcome

items.content_statement: Ethical behaviour goes beyond consent and involves kindness, care, attention to the needs and vulnerabilities of the other person, as well as an awareness of power dynamics
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about what constitutes ethical behaviour - a conceptual understanding that can be explained and assessed through clear criteria

items.content_statement: Just because someone says yes to doing something, that doesn't automatically make it ethically ok
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the relationship between consent and ethics - a conceptual principle that can be understood and assessed

items.content_statement: How stereotypes, in particular stereotypes based on sex, gender reassignment, race, religion, sexual orientation or disability, can cause damage (e.g. how they might normalise non-consensual behaviour or encourage prejudice)
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how stereotypes function and their effects

items.content_statement: Recognise misogyny and other forms of prejudice
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for identifying prejudice - occasion-triggered skill
prerequisite_lts: How stereotypes, in particular stereotypes based on sex, gender reassignment, race, religion, sexual orientation or disability, can cause damage

items.content_statement: How inequalities of power can impact behaviour within relationships, including sexual relationships. For example, how people who are disempowered can feel they are not entitled to be treated with respect by others or how those who enjoy an unequal amount of power might, with or without realising it, impose their preferences on others.
items.classification_rationale: This describes conceptual understanding of how power dynamics function in relationships - a transferable concept that can be understood and explained, fitting Type 1 conceptual knowledge.

items.content_statement: How pornography can negatively influence sexual attitudes and behaviours by normalising harmful sexual behaviours and by disempowering some people, especially women, to feel a sense of autonomy over their own body and providing some people with a sense of sexual entitlement to the bodies of others
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how pornography affects attitudes and behaviours through specific mechanisms

items.content_statement: Discuss how some sub-cultures might influence our understanding of sexual ethics, including the sexual norms endorsed by so-called "involuntary celibates" (incels) or online influencers
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when discussing sexual ethics - requires reasoning across multiple perspectives about cultural influence, no single correct answer but better and worse analyses

items.content_statement: The same expectations of behaviour apply in all contexts, including online
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - a conceptual principle about behavioral consistency across contexts that learners must understand

items.content_statement: the importance of being cautious about sharing personal information online and of using privacy and location settings appropriately to protect information online
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about online safety principles and protective measures

items.content_statement: the difference between public and private online spaces and related safety issues
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding of online space distinctions and their safety implications

items.content_statement: Social media accounts may be fake and may post things which aren't real or have been created with AI
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how social media works - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: Social media users may say things in more extreme ways than they might in face-to-face situations
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about social media behavior patterns - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: Some social media users present highly exaggerated or idealised profiles of themselves online
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about online self-presentation - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: Any material provided online might be circulated, and that once this has happened there is no way of controlling where it ends up
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how digital sharing works - a factual claim about the nature of online distribution

items.content_statement: The serious risks of sending material to others, including the law concerning the sharing of images
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal and personal risks - factual content about consequences and legal frameworks

items.content_statement: Not to provide material to others that they would not want to be distributed further and not to pass on personal material which is sent to them
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation - a default practice of respecting privacy and boundaries in digital sharing that operates across contexts
prerequisite_lts: Any material provided online might be circulated, and that once this has happened there is no way of controlling where it ends up
The serious risks of sending material to others, including the law concerning the sharing of images

items.content_statement: That keeping or forwarding indecent or sexual images of someone under 18 is a crime, even if the photo is of themselves or of someone who has consented, and even if the image was created by the child and/or using AI generated imagery
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal facts - factual content that must be held

items.content_statement: The potentially serious consequences of acquiring or generating indecent or sexual images of someone under 18, including the potential for criminal charges and severe penalties including imprisonment
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of consequences and implications - transferable conceptual ideas

items.content_statement: How to seek support and that they will not be in trouble for asking for help, either at school or with the police, if an image of themselves has been shared
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about support procedures and protections - factual content that must be held

items.content_statement: That sharing indecent images of people over 18 without consent is a crime
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal facts - factual content that must be held

items.content_statement: What to do and how to report when they are concerned about material that has been circulated, including personal information, images or videos, and how to manage issues online
items.classification_rationale: Describes analytical capability deployed when a situation calls for it - responding to concerning online material requires reasoning about context, severity, appropriate reporting channels, and management strategies

items.content_statement: the prevalence of deepfakes including videos and photos
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about factual information regarding deepfake prevalence

items.content_statement: how deepfakes can be used maliciously as well as for entertainment
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding of deepfake applications and purposes

items.content_statement: the harms that can be caused by deepfakes
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding of deepfake consequences and impacts

items.content_statement: how to identify deepfakes
items.classification_rationale: analytical skill deployed when encountering potential deepfake content

items.content_statement: That the internet contains inappropriate and upsetting content, some of which is illegal, including unacceptable content that encourages misogyny, violence or use of weapons
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the nature of internet content - factual claim about how the world works that learners must understand

items.content_statement: Where to go for advice and support about something they have seen online
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual information about support resources that must be known

items.content_statement: That online content can present a distorted picture of the world and normalise or glamorise behaviours which are unhealthy and wrong
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding - causal claim about how online content affects perception that learners must internalise

items.content_statement: How to identify when technology and social media is used as part of bullying, harassment, stalking, coercive and controlling behaviour, and other forms of abusive and/or illegal behaviour and how to seek support about concerns.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - recognising patterns of harmful behaviour across digital contexts and determining appropriate support responses

items.content_statement: That pornography, and other online content, often presents a distorted picture of people and their sexual behaviours and can negatively affect how people behave towards sexual partners. This can affect pupils who see pornographic content accidentally as well as those who see it deliberately. Pornography can also portray misogynistic behaviours and attitudes which can negatively influence those who see it.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how pornography affects behaviour and attitudes. This is factual/conceptual content about how the world works that learners must understand.

items.content_statement: That websites may share personal data about their users, and information collected on their internet use, for commercial purposes (e.g. to enable targeted advertising).
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how websites operate commercially - factual content about data sharing practices that learners must understand

items.content_statement: That criminals can operate online scams, for example using fake websites or emails to extort money or valuable personal information. This information can be used to the detriment of the person or wider society.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how online scams operate and their consequences - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: About risks of sextortion
items.classification_rationale: Factual knowledge about specific risks - propositional content to be understood

items.content_statement: how to identify online scams relating to sex
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when encountering potential scams - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning quality

items.content_statement: how to seek support if they have been scammed or involved in sextortion
items.classification_rationale: Capability deployed when specific situations arise requiring support-seeking - occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: AI chatbots are an example of how AI is rapidly developing, and that these can pose risks by creating fake intimacy or offering harmful advice
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about AI chatbots and their specific risks - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: critically think about new types of technology as they appear online and how they might pose a risk
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when encountering new technology - occasion-triggered reasoning skill across multiple contexts

items.content_statement: How to recognise, respect and communicate consent and boundaries in relationships, including in early romantic relationships (in all contexts, including online) and early sexual relationships that might involve kissing or touching
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when relationship situations call for it - multiple valid approaches to recognising and communicating boundaries across different contexts

items.content_statement: That kindness and care for others requires more than just consent
items.classification_rationale: Propositional claim about how relationships work - conceptual understanding that can be explained and applied

items.content_statement: how to determine whether other children, adults or sources of information are trustworthy, how to judge when a relationship is unsafe (and recognise this in the relationships of others)
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when situations call for judgment about trustworthiness and safety - multiple valid frameworks for assessment, reasoning quality determines outcome

items.content_statement: how to seek help or advice, including reporting concerns about others, if needed
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when situations call for help-seeking decisions - involves judgment about when and how to act

items.content_statement: Ways of seeking help when needed and how to report harmful behaviour
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about specific procedures and resources - hard prerequisite sequence

items.content_statement: That there are strategies they can use to increase their safety, and that this does not mean they will be blamed if they are victims of harmful behaviour
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding about safety strategies and victim-blaming - propositional knowledge

items.content_statement: That in some situations a person might appear trustworthy but have harmful intentions
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding about deceptive appearances - propositional knowledge

items.content_statement: How to increase their personal safety in public spaces, including when socialising with friends, family, the wider community or strangers
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when specific situations arise - occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: Trusting their instincts when something doesn't feel right
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientation that operates across contexts - dispositional capacity

items.content_statement: Sexual harassment and sexual violence are unacceptable behaviours, and it is never the fault of the person experiencing it.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual claims about the nature of sexual harassment/violence and responsibility attribution that learners must understand

items.content_statement: Sexual harassment includes unsolicited sexual language / attention / touching, taking and/or sharing intimate or sexual images without consent, public sexual harassment, pressuring other people to do sexual things, and upskirting.
items.classification_rationale: Definitional content - propositional knowledge about what constitutes sexual harassment with specific examples

items.content_statement: sexual harassment and sexual violence among young people but also includes other forms of concerning behaviour like using age-inappropriate sexual language
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about what constitutes harmful sexual behaviour - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: The concepts and laws relating to domestic abuse, including controlling or coercive behaviour, emotional, sexual, economic or physical abuse, and violent or threatening behaviour
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal concepts and definitions that must be understood - hard prerequisite sequence applies as legal understanding builds on foundational concepts

items.content_statement: The concepts and laws relating to harms which are exploitative, including sexual exploitation, criminal exploitation and abuse, grooming, and financial exploitation
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal concepts and definitions that must be understood - hard prerequisite sequence applies as legal understanding builds on foundational concepts

items.content_statement: The Domestic Abuse Act 2021 recognised children who see, hear, or experience the effects of abuse, and are related to either the victim of the abusive behaviour, or the perpetrator, as victims of domestic abuse in their own right (part 1 section 3)
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal definitions and statutory recognition - factual content about what the Act established

items.content_statement: The Domestic Abuse Act 2021 statutory guidance is designed to support statutory and non-statutory bodies working with victims of domestic abuse, including children
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the purpose and scope of statutory guidance - factual content about what the guidance is designed to do

items.content_statement: It is illegal to assist in the performance of FGM, virginity testing or hymenoplasty, in the UK or abroad, or to fail to protect a person under 16 for whom they are responsible
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal facts - hard prerequisite sequence applies as legal knowledge builds on understanding of what constitutes these practices

items.content_statement: Strangulation (applying pressure to the neck) is an offence, regardless of whether it causes injury
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual claim about legal status that learners must hold

items.content_statement: That any activity that involves applying force or pressure to someone's neck or covering someone's mouth and nose is dangerous and can lead to serious injury or death.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual claim about causal relationship between specific actions and their consequences

items.content_statement: That pornography presents some activities as normal which many people do not and will never engage in, some of which can be emotionally and/or physically harmful.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about pornography's misrepresentation of sexual norms and potential harms - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: How to seek support for their own worrying or abusive behaviour or for worrying or abusive behaviour they have experienced from others, including information on where to report abuse, and where to seek medical attention when required, for example after an assault.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about specific procedures and information sources - hard prerequisite sequence applies as learners must know the factual information before they can apply it

items.content_statement: Sex, for people who feel ready and are over the age of consent, can and should be enjoyable and positive
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - a factual/value claim about the nature of sexual experience that learners must understand

items.content_statement: that many young people wait until they are older, and that people of all ages can enjoy intimate and romantic relationships without sex
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about relationship patterns and possibilities - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: Sexual consent can be given, withheld or removed at any time, even if initially given
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how consent works - factual content about consent's revocable nature

items.content_statement: Considerations that people might take into account prior to sexual activity include the law, faith and family values
items.classification_rationale: Factual knowledge about factors that inform sexual decision-making

items.content_statement: Kindness and care for others require more than just consent
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding about the relationship between consent and broader ethical considerations in relationships

items.content_statement: That all aspects of health can be affected by choices they make in sex and relationships, positively or negatively, e.g. physical, emotional, mental, sexual and reproductive health and wellbeing.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how choices affect health outcomes across multiple domains

items.content_statement: The facts about the full range of contraceptive choices, efficacy and options available, including male and female condoms
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about contraceptive facts, efficacy, and options - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: Signposting towards medically accurate online information about sexual and reproductive health to support contraceptive decision-making
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - evaluating and directing others to appropriate information sources for decision-making
prerequisite_lts: The facts about the full range of contraceptive choices, efficacy and options available, including male and female condoms

items.content_statement: medically and legally accurate and impartial information on all pregnancy options, including keeping the baby, adoption, abortion and where to get further help
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about pregnancy options - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: How risk can be reduced through safer sex including through condom use and the use and availability of HIV prevention drugs Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP) and Post-Exposure Prophylaxis
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about specific risk reduction methods and medical interventions - factual content that can be learned and assessed through knowledge checks

items.content_statement: Exposure Prophylaxis (PEP) and how and where to access them
items.classification_rationale: Factual knowledge about what PEP is and access procedures - propositional content with no prerequisite sequence indicated

items.content_statement: The importance of, and facts about, regular testing
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of why regular testing matters and factual knowledge about testing - propositional content

items.content_statement: The role of stigma
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of how stigma functions in this context - propositional content about causal relationships

items.content_statement: The prevalence of STIs, the short and long term impact they can have on those who contract them and key facts about treatment.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual content about STI prevalence, impacts, and treatment facts that learners must hold

items.content_statement: How and where to seek support for concerns around sexual relationships including sexual violence or harms
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about support resources and procedures - factual content that can be learned and recalled

items.content_statement: Where to access confidential sexual and reproductive health advice and treatment
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about specific resources and access points - factual content that can be directly assessed

items.content_statement: How to counter misinformation about sexual and reproductive health, including signposting towards medically accurate information and further advice
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when encountering misinformation - requires reasoning about information quality and appropriate response strategies

items.content_statement: The benefits of physical activity, time outdoors, and helping others for health, wellbeing and happiness
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - factual claims about what benefits health and wellbeing

items.content_statement: Simple self-care techniques, including the importance of rest, time spent with friends and family, as well as hobbies, interests and community participation
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about self-care techniques and their importance - factual content about what constitutes self-care

items.content_statement: The range and scale of emotions (e.g. happiness, sadness, anger, fear, surprise, nervousness) that they might experience in different situations
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about emotional categories and their situational contexts - factual content to be held

items.content_statement: That worrying and feeling down are normal, affect everyone at different times, and are not in themselves a sign of a mental health condition
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding about the normalcy of certain emotional experiences - transferable principle about mental health

items.content_statement: How to recognise feelings and use varied vocabulary to talk about their own and others' feelings
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for emotional recognition and communication - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning about emotional states

items.content_statement: How to judge whether what they are feeling and how they are behaving is appropriate and proportionate
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for self-evaluation - involves reasoning about appropriateness across multiple contexts rather than fixed prerequisite knowledge

items.content_statement: That bullying (including cyberbullying) has a negative and often lasting impact on mental wellbeing
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how bullying affects mental wellbeing

items.content_statement: How to seek help for themselves or others when experiencing bullying impacts on mental wellbeing
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for help-seeking - reasoning about appropriate responses and resources
prerequisite_lts: That bullying (including cyberbullying) has a negative and often lasting impact on mental wellbeing

items.content_statement: that grief is a natural response to bereavement, and that everyone grieves differently
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about the nature of grief - factual claims about how grief works that learners must understand

items.content_statement: Who in school they should speak to if they are worried about their own or someone else's mental wellbeing or ability to control their emotions (including issues arising online)
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about specific people and procedures - factual content that can be directly assessed

items.content_statement: Recognising the triggers for seeking support for mental wellbeing concerns
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - recognising patterns and making judgments about when support is needed

items.content_statement: Think about positive and negative aspects of the internet
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when considering internet use - multiple valid frameworks for analysis, reasoning quality determines outcome

items.content_statement: discuss how online relationships can complement and support meaningful in-person relationships, but also how they might be in tension, and the reasons why online relationships are unlikely to be a good substitute for high quality in-person relationships, looking at the pros and cons of different ways of using online connection
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when situations call for discussion of relationship dynamics - multiple valid frameworks for analysis, reasoning quality determines outcome

items.content_statement: The benefits of limiting time spent online, the risks of excessive time spent on electronic devices and the impact of positive and negative content online on their own and others' mental and physical wellbeing
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how online time and content affect wellbeing. This is factual/conceptual content to be understood, not a skill to deploy or orientation to sustain.

items.content_statement: recognise and display respectful behaviour online
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability (recognising respectful behaviour) and occasion-prompted deployment (displaying it when online), not a sustained default orientation

items.content_statement: Why social media, some apps, computer games and online gaming, including gambling sites, are age restricted
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about factual reasons for age restrictions - can be assessed through knowledge test

items.content_statement: The risks relating to online gaming, video game monetisation, scams, fraud and other financial harms, and that gaming can become addictive
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about specific risks and facts that learners must hold - hard prerequisite sequence applies as understanding these risks is foundational

items.content_statement: Take a critical approach to what they see and read online and make responsible decisions about which content, including content on social media and apps, is appropriate for them
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when encountering online content - involves evaluating through multiple lenses (safety, appropriateness, reliability) with reasoning quality determining outcome

items.content_statement: That abuse, bullying and harassment can take place online and that this can impact wellbeing
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - factual content about how online abuse affects wellbeing

items.content_statement: How to seek support from trusted adults
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered skill - analytical capability deployed when a situation calls for seeking support

items.content_statement: know how information is selected and targeted
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about how information systems work - factual content that can be taught and assessed through knowledge checks

items.content_statement: how to achieve building regular physical activity into daily and weekly routines; for example, walking or cycling to school, a daily active mile or other forms of regular, moderate and/or vigorous physical activity
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about methods and strategies for incorporating physical activity - factual content about how to achieve the goal

items.content_statement: which adults to speak to in school if they are worried about their health
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about specific factual information - who the designated adults are in their school context

items.content_statement: how and when to seek support when worried about their health
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when a health concern situation arises - requires reasoning about appropriateness of timing and method

items.content_statement: nutritional content including understanding calories and other nutritional content
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about factual content - what calories are, what other nutritional components exist, and their properties

items.content_statement: The characteristics of a poor diet and risks associated with unhealthy eating including obesity and tooth decay and other behaviours such as the impact of alcohol on diet or health
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about health risks and causal relationships - factual content about how diet affects health outcomes

items.content_statement: The facts about legal and illegal harmful substances and associated risks, including smoking, vaping, alcohol use and drug-taking, including the risks of nicotine addiction caused by nicotine products such as nicotine pouches
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about factual content regarding substances and their risks - hard prerequisite sequence applies as understanding basic facts about substances must precede understanding their specific risks and effects

items.content_statement: Early signs of physical illness, such as weight loss or unexplained changes to the body
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about factual indicators - specific signs that can be learned and recognized

items.content_statement: About safe and unsafe exposure to the sun, and how to reduce the risk of sun damage, including skin cancer.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about sun safety and health risks - factual content that can be learned and assessed through knowledge checks

items.content_statement: recommended amount of sleep for their age
items.classification_rationale: factual information about sleep requirements - propositional knowledge

items.content_statement: practical steps for improving sleep, such as not using screens in the bedroom
items.classification_rationale: factual knowledge of specific sleep hygiene practices

items.content_statement: the impact of poor sleep on weight, mood and ability to learn
items.classification_rationale: causal understanding of how sleep affects multiple health outcomes

items.content_statement: twice a day with fluoride toothpaste, cleaning between teeth, and regular check-ups at the dentist
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about specific dental hygiene practices - factual content that can be assessed through knowledge check

items.content_statement: About personal hygiene and germs including bacteria, viruses, how they are spread and treated, and the importance of handwashing.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about factual content - what germs are, how they spread, how they are treated, and why handwashing is important. This is factual/conceptual content that can be assessed through knowledge checks.

items.content_statement: The introduction of topics relating to vaccination and immunisation should be aligned with when vaccinations are offered to pupils
items.classification_rationale: This is propositional knowledge about curriculum sequencing - a factual claim about how vaccination education should be timed relative to actual vaccination schedules

items.content_statement: About hazards (including fire risks) that may cause harm, injury or risk and ways to reduce risks
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about hazards and risk reduction methods - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: How to recognise risk and keep safe around roads, railways, including level crossings, and water, including the water safety code
items.classification_rationale: Hard prerequisite sequence - specific safety knowledge must be understood before it can be applied

items.content_statement: the importance of reporting incidents rather than filming them
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about appropriate emergency response behavior - factual content about what should be prioritized

items.content_statement: Concepts of basic first aid for head injuries
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about first aid concepts that must be understood - Type 1 factual/conceptual content

items.content_statement: The human lifecycle and puberty as a stage in this process, including growth and other ways the body can change and develop during adolescence
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about biological processes and developmental stages - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: The correct names of body parts, including the penis, vulva, vagina, testicles, scrotum, nipples
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual content about anatomical terminology that must be held

items.content_statement: All of these parts of the body are private
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding - transferable idea about the private nature of specific body parts
prerequisite_lts: The correct names of body parts, including the penis, vulva, vagina, testicles, scrotum, nipples

items.content_statement: Skills to understand and express their own boundaries around these body parts
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered analytical skill - capability deployed when situations call for understanding or expressing personal boundaries
prerequisite_lts: All of these parts of the body are private

items.content_statement: The average age of the onset of menstruation is twelve, periods can start at eight
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual claims about menstrual cycle timing that must be understood

items.content_statement: Covering menstruation before girls' periods start helps them understand what to expect and avoid distress
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding - causal relationship between timing of education and emotional outcomes

items.content_statement: understand their changing bodies and their feelings
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about bodily changes and emotional development that must be understood

items.content_statement: how to protect their own health and wellbeing
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when health and wellbeing decisions arise

items.content_statement: when a physical or mental health issue requires attention
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when evaluating whether health issues need professional attention

items.content_statement: recognising and talking about emotions
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about emotional recognition and vocabulary that must be understood

items.content_statement: looking after one's own and others' wellbeing
items.classification_rationale: sustained default orientation of caring for wellbeing that operates across contexts
prerequisite_lts: recognising and talking about emotions

items.content_statement: judging whether feelings or behaviour require support
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when situations call for assessment of support needs

items.content_statement: how to cope when things go wrong in life
items.classification_rationale: analytical and reasoning capability deployed when difficult situations arise

items.content_statement: how to seek help from a trusted adult, including when they are concerned about another person
items.classification_rationale: procedural knowledge about help-seeking processes that must be understood

items.content_statement: Staff need evidence-based training before addressing suicide directly with secondary aged pupils to ensure they have the knowledge and skills to do this safely
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about training requirements - factual content about what must be in place

items.content_statement: Use language and content that is accurate, straightforward and appropriate when addressing suicide with pupils
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when the situation calls for addressing suicide - requires judgment about appropriateness across contexts
prerequisite_lts: Staff need evidence-based training before addressing suicide directly with secondary aged pupils to ensure they have the knowledge and skills to do this safely

items.content_statement: How to talk about their emotions accurately and sensitively, using appropriate vocabulary
items.classification_rationale: Describes analytical capability deployed when situations call for emotional communication - requires reasoning about context, audience, and appropriate expression choices

items.content_statement: participation and volunteering or acts of kindness for mental wellbeing and happiness
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how specific activities affect mental wellbeing

items.content_statement: understand what makes them feel happy and what makes them feel unhappy
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about personal emotional triggers - factual self-knowledge that can be understood and explained

items.content_statement: recognising that loneliness can be for most people an inevitable part of life at times and is not something of which to be ashamed
items.classification_rationale: propositional understanding about the nature of loneliness as a normal human experience - conceptual knowledge about emotional normalcy

items.content_statement: That worrying and feeling down are normal, can affect everyone at different times and are not in themselves a sign of a mental health condition, and that managing those feelings can be helped by seeing them as normal.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the nature of normal emotional experiences and their relationship to mental health conditions - factual/conceptual content to be understood

items.content_statement: Factual information about the prevalence and characteristics of common types of mental ill health and more serious mental health conditions
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual content about prevalence and characteristics that learners must hold

items.content_statement: The distinction between normal feelings and mental health conditions
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding - transferable idea about appropriate categorization that learners must internalize

items.content_statement: It's possible to overcome barriers to participating in fun, enjoyable or rewarding activities using coping strategies, and that finding the courage to participate in activities which initially feel challenging may decrease anxiety over time rather than increasing it
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how anxiety and participation work - causal claims about what happens when people use coping strategies and engage with challenging activities

items.content_statement: That gambling can lead to serious mental health harms, including anxiety, depression, and suicide, and that some gambling products are more likely to cause these harms than others
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - causal claims about how gambling affects mental health that learners must understand as factual content

items.content_statement: That the co-occurrence of alcohol/drug use and poor mental health is common and that the relationship is bi-directional: mental health problems can increase the risk of alcohol/drug use, and alcohol/drug use can trigger mental health problems or exacerbate existing ones
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - factual claims about how mental health and substance use interact

items.content_statement: That stopping smoking can improve people's mental health and decrease anxiety
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - factual claim about effects of smoking cessation on mental health

items.content_statement: About the benefits of limiting time spent online, the risks of excessive time spent on electronic devices and the impact of positive and negative content online on their own and others' mental and physical wellbeing
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how online time and content affect wellbeing. This is factual/conceptual content to be understood, not a skill to deploy or orientation to sustain.

items.content_statement: The impact of unhealthy or obsessive comparison with others online including through setting unrealistic expectations for body image
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how online comparison affects wellbeing and self-image

items.content_statement: How people may curate a specific image of their life online
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how online presentation works - factual understanding of digital behavior patterns

items.content_statement: The impact that an over-reliance on online relationships, including relationships formed through social media, can have
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how over-reliance on digital relationships affects wellbeing and social development

items.content_statement: how to report, or find support, if they have been affected by harmful behaviours online
items.classification_rationale: occasion-triggered analytical capability - deployed when a situation calls for it (when affected by harmful online behaviours)

items.content_statement: The risks related to online gambling and gambling-like content within gaming, including the accumulation of debt
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about specific risks and consequences - factual content about what risks exist and their effects

items.content_statement: understanding the prevalence of misinformation and disinformation online, including conspiracy theories
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about how misinformation/disinformation operates online - factual content about prevalence and types

items.content_statement: how to be a discerning consumer of information online
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when encountering information online - reasoning quality determines assessment outcome
prerequisite_lts: understanding the prevalence of misinformation and disinformation online, including conspiracy theories

items.content_statement: how advertising and information is targeted at them
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about targeting mechanisms - factual content about how advertising/information targeting works

items.content_statement: The risks of illegal behaviours online, including drug and knife supply or the sale or purchasing of illicit drugs online
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about specific risks and illegal activities - factual content that learners must hold

items.content_statement: The serious risks of viewing online content that promotes self-harm, suicide or violence
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about specific risks - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: How to safely report material that promotes self-harm, suicide or violence
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered analytical skill deployed when encountering harmful content

items.content_statement: How to access support after viewing content that promotes self-harm, suicide or violence
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered analytical skill deployed when support is needed after exposure

items.content_statement: the characteristics of a healthy lifestyle, including physical activity and maintaining a healthy weight, including the links between an inactive lifestyle and ill-health, including cardiovascular ill-health
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about health relationships and causal links - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: the links between a poor diet and health risks, including tooth decay, unhealthy weight gain, and cardiovascular disease
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about causal relationships between diet and specific health outcomes - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: Unhealthy weight gain increases risks of cancer, type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about health risks - factual content that can be assessed through knowledge test

items.content_statement: The facts about which drugs are illegal, the risks of taking illegal drugs, including the increased risk of potent synthetic drugs being added to illegal drugs, the risks of illicit vapes containing drugs, illicit drugs and counterfeit medicines, and the potential health harms, including the link to poor mental health.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual content about drug classifications, risks, and health consequences that learners must hold

items.content_statement: What constitutes low risk alcohol consumption in adulthood, and the legal age of sale for alcohol in England
items.classification_rationale: Factual propositional knowledge - specific definitions and legal facts that can be directly assessed

items.content_statement: Understanding how to increase personal safety while drinking alcohol, including how to decrease the risks of having a drink spiked or of poisoning from potentially fatal substances such as methanol
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of safety principles and risk reduction strategies - hierarchical knowledge that builds on factual understanding of alcohol risks

items.content_statement: alcohol dependency
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about a specific condition - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: The facts about the multiple serious harms from smoking tobacco (particularly the link to lung cancer and cardiovascular disease), the benefits of quitting and how to access support to do so
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about health facts, causal relationships, and support resources - hard prerequisite sequence not required
