items.content_statement: The facts about vaping, including the harms posed to young people, and the role that vapes can play in helping adult smokers to quit
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual claims about vaping harms and smoking cessation role that learners must hold

items.content_statement: treatment and prevention of infection, and about antibiotics
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about medical facts and procedures that can be learned and assessed through knowledge checks

items.content_statement: Dental health and the benefits of good oral hygiene, including brushing teeth twice a day with fluoride toothpaste and cleaning between teeth, reducing consumption of sugar-containing food and drinks, and regular check-ups at the dentist
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about dental health practices and their benefits - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: knowledgeable healthcare professionals
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about the role/status of pharmacists as healthcare professionals

items.content_statement: the benefits of regular self-examination and screening
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about health benefits - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: taking responsibility for their own health through regular self-examination and screening
items.classification_rationale: sustained orientation - responsibility enacted as default practice over time
prerequisite_lts: the benefits of regular self-examination and screening

items.content_statement: The facts and scientific evidence relating to vaccination, immunisation and antimicrobial resistance
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about scientific facts and evidence - hard prerequisite sequence exists for understanding scientific concepts

items.content_statement: screen-free time before bed and removing phones from the bedroom are important for good-quality sleep
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about sleep hygiene practices - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: how a lack of sleep can affect weight, mood and ability to learn
items.classification_rationale: causal understanding of sleep's effects on multiple health and learning outcomes - conceptual knowledge

items.content_statement: The importance of pre-conception health, including taking folic acid
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about health facts and recommendations that learners must understand

items.content_statement: The importance of pelvic floor health
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about health facts that learners must understand

items.content_statement: Information on miscarriage and pregnancy loss, and how to access care and support
items.classification_rationale: Factual knowledge about health conditions and support systems that learners must know

items.content_statement: what a GP is; when to use A&E / minor injuries; accessing sexual health and family planning clinics; the role of local pharmacies; and how to seek help via local third sector partners which may have specialist services
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about healthcare system components and their functions - factual content that can be learned and assessed through knowledge checks

items.content_statement: Before age 16, a child's parents will have responsibility for consenting to medical treatment on their behalf unless they are Gillick competent to take this decision for themselves
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal framework - factual content about how medical consent works for minors

items.content_statement: Pupils should understand the circumstances in which someone over 16 may not be deemed to have capacity to make decisions about medical treatment.
items.classification_rationale: This is propositional knowledge about legal/medical concepts that learners must understand - specific factual content about capacity and medical consent that can be assessed through explanation or knowledge checks.

items.content_statement: identify risk and manage personal safety in increasingly independent situations, including around roads, railways – including level crossings - and water (including the water safety code), and in unfamiliar social or work settings (for example the first time a young person goes on holiday without their parents)
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - assessing risk and managing safety requires reasoning across multiple contexts and frameworks, with quality of analysis determining effectiveness

items.content_statement: How to recognise and manage peer influence in relation to risk-taking behaviour and personal safety, including peer influence online and on social media
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations involving peer influence arise - requires reasoning across multiple contexts and scenarios

items.content_statement: Skills to support self-awareness, self-management, social awareness, relationship skills and responsible decision making in contexts involving conflict and violence
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capabilities deployed when situations call for them - these are occasion-triggered skills for navigating social situations

items.content_statement: Skills to recognise and manage peer pressure
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when peer pressure situations arise - occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: Carrying weapons is uncommon among young people
items.classification_rationale: Propositional/factual content about statistical reality that learners must hold as knowledge

items.content_statement: Misconceptions about weapon prevalence can lead to the false belief that carrying a knife is necessary for protection
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of causal relationship between perception and behavior that learners must internalize

items.content_statement: seek help where there is a concern about grooming or exploitation

These topics can be taught about in secondary schools in a safe and sensitive way but are specialised areas and schools should use qualified support or advice, ensure that they are using reliable high-quality teaching material, and signpost to external support as needed. Schools may consider accessing support from the NHS or local specialist services who may be able to provide advice and CPD for teachers. It is important that schools ensure that teachers are properly equipped to lead discussions about these topics, including what to do if a pupil makes a disclosure.
